LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MAY-20-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'S MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS, RESTRAINING ORDER AND TURNOVER ORDER; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS, RESTRAINING ORDER, AND TURNOVER ORDER WITHOUT PREJUDICE. JUDGMENT CREDITOR OBTAINED A DEFAULT JUDGMENT ON APRIL 18, 2025 AND MAKES NO SHOWING THAT THE JUDGMENT HAS BEEN SERVED OR THAT ANY DEMAND FOR PAYMENT OF THE JUDGMENT WAS MADE. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1213. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
Off Calendar Jul-01-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion for assignment of rights, restraining order, and turnover order is granted. Moving party shall prepare a proposed order that specifies the relief sought and granted as set out in the notice of motion.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
Off Calendar May-29-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court denies the motion for assignment of rights, restraining order, and turnover order without prejudice. Judgment creditor obtained a default judgment on April 18, 2025 and makes no showing that the judgment has been served or that any demand for payment of the judgment was made.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1213.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

585(A)(&)(B)
Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant Philip Boynton Miner.

Dept.505: Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: D. Carter; Proceedings reported by "For the Record", commencing at 9:45am a.m. and concluding at 9:58 a.m. (D505)

David Cook, Esq. representing Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc. via Zoom Video Conference.
Witness for the Plaintiff Marco Padilla appeared via zoom, placed under oath, and testified.
Defendant Philip boyunton Miner did not appear.

After review of the pleadings and testimony of counsel and Plaintiff, the court grants the following judgmnet:

The Plaintiff is to recover from the Defendant as follows:
Damages $31,744.75
Interest $0
Attorney's Fees 1,000.00
Costs $697.26
Total Judgment $33442.01
Proposed judgment as amended signed and executed this day.
